steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Puranik's research shows that interruptions produce social benefits (sense of belonging, job satisfaction) that help both employees and employers—the decisive finding that contradicts the opening concern about productivity.

steps.title: Match the answer to the finding
steps.content:
A correctly states that interpersonal benefits can offset the perceived negative effects, capturing the research outcome that challenges the initial belief.

  • B prescribes frequent interruptions beyond what the research supports
  • C is unrelated to organizational benefits
  • D directly contradicts Puranik's finding
.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
